# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple Dockerfile for our Go microservice. Compile the application and run the binary. Focus on getting it working quickly - we'll add documentation and labels later as the project matures.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security


# Use an official Golang runtime image in your Docker container, e.g., golang:1.8
FROM golang:latest  # replace with latest version of Go you want to use for the microservice application

WORKDIR /app   # set working directory inside docker container at location '/app' on host machine (default) or any other path specified in WORKDIR instruction, e.g., "/home/user" -> COPY --from=0 /src/. .  // replace with your source code folder

# Copy the Go application binary into our Docker image and set execute permissions for it to be able to run when container is started (replace 'your-app' below)   e.g., RUN cp $GOPATH/src/github.com/user/myApp /usr/local/bin
COPY ./cmd/$GO_APP/. .  # replace with your application binary file and directory, for example: COPY --from=0 github.com/username/your-app myApp   // copy the Go app from source to destination inside Docker image (replace 'myApp' below)
RUN chmod +x $GO_APP    # set execute permissions on your binary file in docker container, replace with name of application you want to run when starting up.  e.g., RUN go build -o myapp main.go   // change the command as per requirement (replace 'myApp' below)
# expose port for communication and allow all traffic from host machine on that specific exposed ports in Docker container, replace with your application required Ports or use default 80 if not specified otherwise e.g., EXPOSE $PORT   // change the command as per requirement (replace '$PORT' below)
EXPOSE  $GO_APP_PORT    # set port for communication and allow all traffic from host machine on that specific exposed ports in Docker container, replace with your application required Ports or use default 80 if not specified otherwise e.g., EXPOSE $PORT   // change the command as per requirement (replace '$GO_APP_PORT' below)
# Define environment variables for our app here and set them to be available in Docker container, replace with your application required Environment Variables or use default  if not specified otherwise e.g., ENV MYSQL_USER=root   // change the command as per requirement (replace 'MYAPPENV' below)
# Define environment variables for our app here and set them to be available in Docker container, replace with your application required Environment Variables or use default  if not specified otherwise e.g., ENV MYSQL_USER=root   // change the command as per requirement (replace 'MYAPPENV' below)
